title: Add a Robot From the Robot Selection Tab
summary: Add a specific robot from the Robot Selection tab by entering the robot ID, selecting Add, and confirming the system shows a successful add. The source also states that if the robot is already in the system, nothing happens.
when_to_use: Use when a specific robot needs to be added to the system from the Robot Selection tab and the operator has the robot ID.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to remove a robot.
Do not assume the robot was added if no success indication appears.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the Robot Selection tab, enter the robot ID, click Add, and verify whether the system shows a successful add.

safety_notes: The source states that if the robot is already in the system, nothing will happen.
If the system does not show that the robot was added successfully, the source does not provide further recovery steps.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Robot Selection tab
Ability to click the Add button

access_required: Access to the Robot Selection tab
system_preconditions: The operator knows the corresponding robot ID.

visual_references.description: Robot Selection tab training frame showing add/remove workflow, including robot ID entry, Add button, and success confirmation concept.

steps.title: Open the Robot Selection tab
steps.purpose: Navigate to the interface where robot add actions are performed.
steps.expected_result: The Robot Selection tab is visible and ready for robot ID entry and add actions.
steps.healthy_condition: The Robot Selection tab is open and the robot ID field and Add control are available.
steps.failure_condition: The Robot Selection tab is not available or the operator cannot access the add controls.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection tab view showing the robot ID entry field and Add button.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Robot Selection tab cannot be accessed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you want to add a certain robot on this tab over here...
Training slide explaining how to add or remove a robot in the Robot Selection tab using a robot ID.

steps.title: Enter the robot ID
steps.purpose: Specify which robot should be added to the system.
steps.instruction: Enter the corresponding robot ID in the robot ID field.
steps.expected_result: The intended robot ID is entered in the Robot Selection tab.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct robot ID is present in the robot ID field.
steps.failure_condition: The robot ID is unavailable, not entered, or may be incorrect.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot ID entry field in the Robot Selection tab.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the corresponding robot ID is not known.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You type in the robot ID...
Enter the corresponding robot ID, click Add...

steps.title: Click Add
steps.purpose: Submit the add request for the specified robot.
steps.expected_result: The system processes the add request for the entered robot ID.
steps.healthy_condition: The add action is accepted by the interface.
steps.failure_condition: The add action cannot be initiated or no system response is observable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Add button in the Robot Selection tab.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Add button is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You simply click the Add button.
Slide text indicates: enter the corresponding robot ID, click Add...

steps.title: Verify successful add indication
steps.purpose: Confirm whether the system reports that the robot was added successfully.
steps.instruction: Check whether the system shows that the robot was added successfully.
steps.expected_result: The system shows a successful add indication if the robot was added.
steps.healthy_condition: The system explicitly shows that the robot was added successfully.
steps.failure_condition: No success indication appears after the add action.

actions.label: Check add confirmation
actions.exact_action: Check whether the system shows that the robot was added successfully.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System success indication that the robot was added successfully.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the system does not show that the robot was added successfully.
Do not assume the robot was added if no success indication appears.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Once it's added successfully, the system will show that the robot was added successfully.
Successful add actions generate explicit success indications in the system.

steps.title: Interpret no-change result
steps.purpose: Apply the source-provided interpretation when the add action produces no change.
steps.instruction: If no change occurs, note that the source states nothing happens when the robot is already in the system.
steps.expected_result: A no-change result is recognized as the source-described behavior for a robot already in the system.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator recognizes that no action may occur because the robot is already in the system.
steps.failure_condition: The operator assumes the robot was newly added without a success indication or misinterprets the no-change result.

actions.label: Observe no-change behavior
actions.exact_action: If the robot is already in the system, nothing will happen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if no success indication appears and further action is required, because the source provides no additional recovery steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the robot is already in the system, nothing will happen.
Adding an already present robot causes no change.

success_criteria: The robot is added to the system.
The system shows that the robot was added successfully.
If no change occurs, the result is consistent with the source statement that the robot may already be in the system.
healthy_conditions: The corresponding robot ID can be entered.
The Add action can be initiated.
The system explicitly shows that the robot was added successfully.
failure_conditions: The Robot Selection tab cannot be accessed.
The robot ID is not available or cannot be entered.
The Add button cannot be used.
The system does not show that the robot was added successfully.
No further recovery path is provided by the source when success is not shown.
escalation_guidance: If the system does not show that the robot was added successfully, escalate because the source does not provide further recovery steps.
Do not assume the robot was added if no success indication appears.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection tab add workflow, including robot ID entry, Add button, and success confirmation behavior.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: If you want to add a certain robot on this tab over here, you type in the robot ID and you simply click the Add button... If the robot is already in the system, nothing will happen, but once it's added successfully, the system will show that the robot was added successfully.

missing_details: The source does not specify any recovery steps if the add action does not show a success indication.
The source does not specify required permissions beyond access to the Robot Selection tab.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required.
